feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Apps
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Apps Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Issues Migrating from DEV to QAS Using CTMS with SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
When using SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Automation,
I encountered issues migrating from the DEV environment to the QAS environment via CTMS.
It seems the problem is related to the scheme version, as the release process fails.

As a workaround, I tried changing the scheme version before releasing, but I couldn’t find a successful method.
In SAP Build Process Automation, I even had to remove all dependencies to import the package, which is not ideal.

Is there any effective way to handle this migration without removing all dependencies or facing scheme version conflicts?
Any best practices or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SAP Build AppsやSAP Build Process Automationを使用して、CTMSでDEV環境からQAS環境へ移送しようとした際、
scheme versionの影響と思われる問題でリリースがうまくできませんでした。

対策としてscheme versionを変更してリリースを試みましたが、成功する方法が見つかりませんでした。
特にSAP Build Process Automationでは、パッケージをインポートするために依存関係をすべて削除する必要があり、
非常に非効率的です。

依存関係を削除せず、scheme versionの問題を回避して移送する良い方法やベストプラクティスはありますでしょうか？
アドバイスをいただけると幸いです。

entry.title: systemVars.dimensions.viewport.height malfunction
entry.content:
In deployment runtime version 4.18.23 of SAP Build Apps free/Appgyver, systemVars.dimensions.viewport.height isn't working causing keyboard to cover the input field along with half the app screen upon trying to write, as shown in the first 2 screenshots!

The last screenshot shows the proper systemVars.dimensions.viewport.height function using older runtime version 4.15.15!  

Originalviewport malfunctionviewport function

entry.title: SAP BUILD Apps: How to edit a project from the lobby?
entry.content:
I startet the project shown in the Screenshot 
Now I want to edit. How can I do this?

 

thanks regards, 

Mario

entry.title: AppGyver Community Edition - Page Not Found
entry.content:
Hi,

Today while accessing https://www.appgyver.com/ i receive the message: page not found.
Also the community edition page doesn't exist. Url changed? Or platform dismissed?

Thanks for help.

entry.title: SAP Build apps (low-code) source code management
entry.content:
Hi there,

we developed our first SAP Build apps (low-code). I wonder if it is possible to export the projects into a git repository. I know the projects can be downloaded manually, but this has many drawbacks imo. Instead, I would prefer to store the project in a git repo for example.

Why? I'm biased with SAP Build Apps (low-code). It often did not work correctly and we had to unsubscripe and subscribe to it back again. Often it works but at some point we discover lots of different issues, like non-availability of fundamental services. By deleting the subscription and subscribe agian, it is fixed until next time.

Once you unsubscribe, all projects will be lost. This will not be accepted by our users, which spends lots of time to build the apps by themself. 

Is there any option to store the projects in a git? Downloading them manually is not an option at all imo. This can be a time-consuming and error-prone task.

entry.title: SAP Build, HTML5 repository app permission
entry.content:
Hi all,

once I deployed an app to BTP environment, I can see the result in HTML5 repository. Let's say I have hundrets of users on BTP and dozends of HTML5 apps deployed. How can I grant access to dedicated apps? 

If I grant access to role collection containing role Application_Frontend_Viewer, users can access all apps, if they know the links. In most cases, they need to authenticate to backend services. But if the destination has been defined with basic auth and fixed users, I have serious security concerns.

What is the right way securing individual apps?

EDIT: I did some further checks. I can use SAP Build Workzone and it's corresponding role concept to grant access to an individual tile. But this does not affect permission to the app itself. As long as the user "knows" the right link, he can always access the app. How to prevent this?

entry.title: SAP Build Apps: Large Image List Item - example
entry.content:
Good morning,

I wonder how to work with the  "Large Image List Item" binding to a page variable.
The is a sample on https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/codejam-01-homepage..html

But this sample works with a data variable. 

As SAP changed the UI I only find "old" examples. 

I would be very glad if somebody could give a small tutorial.

thanks regards, 

Mario

entry.title: SAP Build low-code, transport 3-tier landscape, governance
entry.content:
Hi all,

our citizen.developers build their first SAP Build Apps (low-code) for UI. It has been build and deployed to BTP environment. Now I'm looking on how to deploy this app to test- and productive subaccount. Ok I can export the project and reimport it into another subaccount but I'm pretty sure it should be handled either by some kind of transport mechanism (CTMS) or CI/CD pipeline. I'm also looking for some governance guidelines.

I found some information about how to do this for SAP Build process automation as well as for SAP Build Apps, pro.code. But I'm looking for Build apps low-code (AppGyver). The resources I found does not fit in our case, as the relevant options are not available to us. For example, in case of SAP Build process automation, such stuff can be configured in "Control Tower" of SAP Build. But in our case, it is pretty empty.

How to manage a 3-tier landscape for SAP Build low-code apps?

entry.title: How to download an image and store it in local device using sap build apps, is it possible ?
entry.content: I have a requirement, where i need to download an image from an existing request which hold image data in backend. and user wants to store the image detail in local device.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps App Editor - Mouse Hover on object
entry.content:
Good morning,

in SAP Build Apps, within the App Editor, I can’t find a Hover action when I move the mouse over an object. I would like to change the background of a container when I hover over it with the mouse.

Thank you,
Marco

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation trigger from SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
I am trying out SAP Build App triggering a Process automation workflow in the Practice system provided by SAP via My Learning

  1. The workflow has its own Form trigger.
  2. The workflow is released, deployed and published to the build app library.
  3. The workflow is tested.
  4. I have added and enabled the workflow in the Integration tab of the Web and Mobile project.

However, when I add the Trigger Workflow flow function, I am not getting the process to select from and instead getting this:

"You have not integrated any processes that can be triggered via API to your project. Please go to the "Integrations" tab to start integrating with SAP Build Process Automation."

 

Am I missing any steps?

entry.title: How use HTTP destination request with GOS?
entry.content:
Hello,
I have an issue that I am facing with a button functionality implemented using the Flow
Function ‘HTTP destination request’ in SAP Build Apps.


Context
I am developing an application in SAP Build Apps that needs to upload attachments to
S/4HANA using the standard OData service API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV.
The upload is triggered by a button press in the UI, and the attachment is selected by the
end user.
I am following an SAP blog that explains how to upload attachments from SAP Build Apps
using the HTTP Destination Request component. In the blog, the implementation is done
using DMS (Document Management Service) and the approach works correctly, including
the CSRF handling and multipart/form-data POST request. In my case, I cannot use DMS
and I need to upload attachments using GOS (Generic Object Services) via the standard
OData service API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV. I am trying to adapt the same approach shown
in the blog to GOS, but this is where I am currently blocked with the 403 Forbidden error.


Source: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/connect-sap-build-apps-with-dm
s-free-projects-videos/ba-p/13986096

I used the same destination configuration as described in the blog.

The only difference in the destination details is the Proxy Type, which is set to ‘Internet’ and in my case is OnPremise.

------

In the Request body i send key: media with value: <picture jpg>

Path: /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/AttachmentContentSet
Headers with the relative value:
● Slug: (Slug of the image)
● LinkedSAPObjectKey: (LinkedSAPObjectKey data from a Notification created
before this)
● BusinessObjectTypeName: BUS2038
● Content-Type: image/jpeg

If I press F12 and look at the network tab I can see the Response, the error 403 Forbidden.

But then If I tried with Postman it works.

And on the S/4HANA usign the transaction /IWFND/TRACES I can see an error exactly on
the moment when a HEAD request is executed


Someone have a clue about this?
Thanks

entry.title: How can we replicate wbs elements from S4 to Fieldglass
entry.content:
We are using SAP Integration Suite to integrate SAP S/4HANA and SAP Fieldglass.

How can we replicate WBS elements from S/4HANA to Fieldglass?
Is there any communication scenario available for this purpose other than SAP_COM_0111?

Additionally, we would like to replicate the Planned Start Date and Planned Finish Date.

However, SAP_COM_0111 does not support these fields.

entry.title: SAP Build App request destination 401
entry.content:
Hi Experts:

I create a SAP Build APP, but trigger process failed for 401

in preview will return 401 to 

{
"status": 401,
"statusText": "Unauthorized",
"headers": {
"cache-control": "no-cache, no-store, max-age=0, must-revalidate",
"expires": "0",
"pragma": "no-cache",
"referrer-policy": "no-referrer",
"www-authenticate": "Bearer error=\"invalid_token\", error_description=\"Error initializing JWT decoder: The token is invalid: Jwt token with audience [destination-xsappname!b9.instance, sb-cloneb3a595c52ed142ec86069eecbc4be8b3!b7280|destination-xsappname!b9, uaa, openid, xs_account, destination-xsappname!b9.subaccount, destination-xsappname!b9] is not issued for these clientIds: [sb-xsuaa!b3591, xsuaa!b3591].\", error_uri=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-3.1\"",
"x-content-type-options": "nosniff",
"x-frame-options": "DENY",
"x-vcap-request-id": "c09776e6-8d3a-4d72-73ec-24a760e9b37b",
"x-xss-protection": "0",
"date": "Fri, 30 Jan 2026 06:45:24 GMT",
"strict-transport-security": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload;",
"connection": "close"
},
"responseText": ""
}
my destination is

in postman, use token get from the clientId and secret in destination, I can call my process successfully

is there someone can help me, thanks a lot!

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

it can work now, maybe it's because there's some delay with the server.

SAP Build SAP Build Apps SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Is SAP Build subscription alone sufficient to create mobile applications, or is SAP Build Apps subsc
entry.content:
As per SAP documentation, SAP Build is a bundle that includes the following services:

  1. SAP Build Apps

  2. SAP Build Process Automation

  3. SAP Build Work Zone

I would like to clarify the licensing and subscription aspect.

If we subscribe only to SAP Build, is that sufficient to create and deploy mobile applications using SAP Build Apps?

Or do we need to purchase/subscribe to SAP Build Apps separately, even though it is part of the SAP Build bundle?

It would be helpful if someone could explain:

Whether SAP Build Apps is automatically included with SAP Build

Or if it requires an individual subscription/entitlement

Any limitations (users, runtime, mobile deployment) with SAP Build Apps under SAP Build

Thanks in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: BPA Unable to verify the signature of the archive (Transport Joule Skills)
entry.content:
Hi guys, could anyone help me out?

In SAP Build Process Automation (BPA), I’m trying to migrate/transport a Joule Skill project from Subaccount A (US, Azure) to Target Subaccount B (another region, US VA, AWS).

The import fails with this error: 

Unable to verify the signature of the archive. Make sure the landscape keys from landscape us21-prod are imported in your landscape, if not, contact DevOps. Ensure that the other potential reasons for this error are checked. This error could happen for the following reasons: Couldn't get key xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx version from credential store.

The same error - Transport and Upload proyect.

 

I’ve searched everywhere and couldn’t find anything useful.

I did find an SAP Note that suggests using a tenant in the same region, which is honestly very limiting because it would force us to change our entire landscape design just because of BPA:

 

 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3472593/E

Has anyone found a way to solve this (e.g., importing landscape keys / fixing credential store key access) and successfully transport BPA/Joule projects across regions?

I already opened a ticket with SAP, but I haven’t received an answer yet.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Clarification on SAP Build Apps Subscription Visibility under build-default Plan
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We need your clarification regarding SAP Build Apps subscription visibility in our SAP BTP subaccount.

Currently, our subaccount has the following subscriptions active under the build-default plan:

SAP Build Process Automation – build-default

SAP Business Application Studio – build-default

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – build-default

As per SAP documentation and licensing understanding, SAP Build Apps is included within the build-default plan and does not appear as a standalone subscription.

However, when we attempt to create a project using Create Project → Web Application (low-code) in SAP Build Apps, the system displays the status as “Not Subscribed”, which creates confusion from an entitlement perspective.

Could you please confirm:

  1. Whether SAP Build Apps entitlement is correctly provided through the build-default plan in our subaccount, and

  2. Why the project creation wizard displays “Not Subscribed” despite the build-default plan being active.

Your confirmation will help us proceed and also communicate the correct information to our stakeholders.

Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: BTP BuildApps- Error handling and logging possibility
entry.content:
Hello community,

Currently,  we are developing Build Apps as mashup/extension for Sales and Service Cloud V2 or C4C V2.

Now we have use case to handle the address validation logic in Build Apps where the external APIs are consumed, that clearly means the APIs could give us different errors/exceptions in case any issue occurred from external system. How can we log and monitor errors occurred in Build Apps from external systems ?

Examples:
Network & Connectivity Errors: Timeouts, DNS failures, and connection resets.
Authentication/Authorization Errors: 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, invalid or expired API keys

Rate Limits and Quotas: Exceeding allowed request volume
Data Format/Schema Mismatches: API changes its data structure
API Provider Downtime (5xx Errors): 500 Internal Server Error (server crash) or 503 Service Unavailable (server overload)
Invalid URL/Endpoint Changes: The provider updates their documentation or removes an endpoint without notice

Can you guide us how to handle errors in Build Apps so that we can take advantage of logging and monitoring?
Thank you.
Willem_Pardaens @vvdries @gregorw @qmacro @martinstenzig

entry.title: Automation around Transports (STMS_IMPORT)
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I am wondering whether somebody from community has "good" experience with automation transport requests in SAP. I am talking about technical transports requests (STMS), not logistics.

In the past I build a process automation with a form where selected users could enter a transport request. The bot checked that request for some preconditions and imported it then into a SAP system.

In the meantime and with much more experience I doubt whether this scenario is really good for a "bot". The check of the preconditions is really complex, because the request is only allowed to have special objects included and nothing else. I am importing with BAPI. There I only can start the import but don't get a reliable feedback (especially for huge requests). Also there are no BAPI for STMS_QA, so I don't get this list clean.

I also noticed that SAP itself doesn't have any automation around STMS_IMPORT in her portfolio (store). That must also have some reasons because there are over 600 projects included and STMS uses nearly every customer.

In the past we dealt that topic with tickets and a manual import from "IT guys".

What are your thoughts about that issue?

entry.title: I want to Create a REST Data Entity using BTP Destination. but unable retrieve expected output.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I want to create a REST Data Entity from my build apps workspace for 'API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV' (/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/) odata service.

I have a BTP destination already configured.

1.Adding BTP Destination in Base section2. adding odata path in the retrieve section3.Test the data entity. got200

4.But not receiving the expected databut when I am retrieving data I am getting below result, it returns status -200. which is OK.

 

But I am not at all expecting this response. It is returning bunch of standard available catalog details. so something is wrong here.

 

Can someone please guide me what is going wrong here.

Is there any specific way I should configure my BTP Destination?
